delegatedBy.description: Capture ideas, tasks, notes, and seeds to their destination

turns.content:
<objective>
Capture ideas, tasks, notes, and seeds to their appropriate destination in the GSD system.

Mode routing:
- **default** (no flag): Capture as a structured todo for later work → add-todo workflow
- **--note**: Zero-friction idea capture (append/list/promote) → note workflow
- **--backlog**: Add an idea to the backlog parking lot (999.x numbering) → add-backlog workflow
- **--seed**: Capture a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions → plant-seed workflow
- **--list**: List pending todos and select one to work on → check-todos workflow
</objective>

<routing>

| Flag | Destination | Workflow |
|------|-------------|----------|
| (none) | Structured todo in .planning/todos/ | add-todo |
| --note | Timestamped note file, list, or promote | note |
| --backlog | ROADMAP.md backlog section (999.x) | add-backlog |
| --seed | .planning/seeds/SEED-NNN-slug.md | plant-seed |
| --list | Interactive todo browser + action router | check-todos |

</routing>

<execution_context>
@/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/add-todo.md
@/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/note.md
@/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/add-backlog.md
@/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/plant-seed.md
@/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/check-todos.md
@/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Arguments: --list

Parse the first token of --list:
- If it is `--note`: strip the flag, pass remainder to note workflow
- If it is `--backlog`: strip the flag, pass remainder to add-backlog workflow
- If it is `--seed`: strip the flag, pass remainder to plant-seed workflow
- If it is `--list`: pass remainder (optional area filter) to check-todos workflow
- Otherwise: pass all of --list to add-todo workflow
</context>

<process>
1. Parse the leading flag (if any) from --list.
2. Load and execute the appropriate workflow end-to-end based on the routing table above.
3. Preserve all workflow gates from the target workflow (directory structure, duplicate detection, commits, etc.).
</process>
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/add-todo.md"}
<path>/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/add-todo.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: <purpose>
2: Capture an idea, task, or issue that surfaces during a GSD session as a structured todo for later work. Enables "thought → capture → continue" flow without losing context.
3: </purpose>
4: 
5: <required_reading>
6: Read all files referenced by the invoking prompt's execution_context before starting.
7: </required_reading>
8: 
9: <process>
10: 
11: <step name="init_context">
12: Load todo context:
13: 
14: ```bash
15: # SDK resolution: prefer local gsd-tools.cjs, fall back to global gsd-sdk (#3668)
16: GSD_TOOLS="${RUNTIME_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)}/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs"
17: if [ -f "$GSD_TOOLS" ]; then
18:   GSD_SDK="node $GSD_TOOLS"
19: elif command -v gsd-sdk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
20:   GSD_SDK="gsd-sdk"
21: else
22:   echo "ERROR: gsd-sdk not found on PATH and $GSD_TOOLS does not exist." >&2
23:   echo "Run: npx get-shit-done-cc@latest --claude --local" >&2
24:   exit 1
25: fi
26: INIT=$($GSD_SDK query init.todos)
27: if [[ "$INIT" == @file:* ]]; then INIT=$(cat "${INIT#@file:}"); fi
28: ```
29: 
30: Extract from init JSON: `commit_docs`, `date`, `timestamp`, `todo_count`, `todos`, `pending_dir`, `todos_dir_exists`.
31: 
32: Ensure directories exist:
33: ```bash
34: mkdir -p .planning/todos/pending .planning/todos/completed
35: ```
36: 
37: Note existing areas from the todos array for consistency in infer_area step.
38: </step>
39: 
40: <step name="extract_content">
41: **With arguments:** Use as the title/focus.
42: - `/gsd-add-todo Add auth token refresh` → title = "Add auth token refresh"
43: 
44: **Without arguments:** Analyze recent conversation to extract:
45: - The specific problem, idea, or task discussed
46: - Relevant file paths mentioned
47: - Technical details (error messages, line numbers, constraints)
48: 
49: Formulate:
50: - `title`: 3-10 word descriptive title (action verb preferred)
51: - `problem`: What's wrong or why this is needed
52: - `solution`: Approach hints or "TBD" if just an idea
53: - `files`: Relevant paths with line numbers from conversation
54: </step>
55: 
56: <step name="infer_area">
57: Infer area from file paths:
58: 
59: | Path pattern | Area |
60: |--------------|------|
61: | `src/api/*`, `api/*` | `api` |
62: | `src/components/*`, `src/ui/*` | `ui` |
63: | `src/auth/*`, `auth/*` | `auth` |
64: | `src/db/*`, `database/*` | `database` |
65: | `tests/*`, `__tests__/*` | `testing` |
66: | `docs/*` | `docs` |
67: | `.planning/*` | `planning` |
68: | `scripts/*`, `bin/*` | `tooling` |
69: | No files or unclear | `general` |
70: 
71: Use existing area from step 2 if similar match exists.
72: </step>
73: 
74: <step name="check_duplicates">
75: ```bash
76: # Search for key words from title in existing todos
77: grep -l -i "[key words from title]" .planning/todos/pending/*.md 2>/dev/null || true
78: ```
79: 
80: If potential duplicate found:
81: 1. Read the existing todo
82: 2. Compare scope
83: 
84: 
85: **Text mode (`workflow.text_mode: true` in config or `--text` flag):** Set `TEXT_MODE=true` if `--text` is present in `$ARGUMENTS` OR `text_mode` from init JSON is `true`. When TEXT_MODE is active, replace every `question` call with a plain-text numbered list and ask the user to type their choice number. This is required for non-the agent runtimes (OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) where `question` is not available.
86: If overlapping, use question:
87: - header: "Duplicate?"
88: - question: "Similar todo exists: [title]. What would you like to do?"
89: - options:
90:   - "Skip" — keep existing todo
91:   - "Replace" — update existing with new context
92:   - "Add anyway" — create as separate todo
93: </step>
94: 
95: <step name="create_file">
96: Use values from init context: `timestamp` and `date` are already available.
97: 
98: Generate slug for the title:
99: ```bash
100: slug=$($GSD_SDK query generate-slug "$title" --raw)
101: ```
102: 
103: Write to `.planning/todos/pending/${date}-${slug}.md`:
104: 
105: ```markdown
106: ---
107: created: [timestamp]
108: title: [title]
109: area: [area]
110: files:
111:   - [file:lines]
112: ---
113: 
114: ## Problem
115: 
116: [problem description - enough context for future the agent to understand weeks later]
117: 
118: ## Solution
119: 
120: [approach hints or "TBD"]
121: ```
122: </step>
123: 
124: <step name="update_state">
125: If `.planning/STATE.md` exists:
126: 
127: 1. Use `todo_count` from init context (or re-run `init todos` if count changed)
128: 2. Update "### Pending Todos" under "## Accumulated Context"
129: </step>
130: 
131: <step name="git_commit">
132: Commit the todo and any updated state:
133: 
134: ```bash
135: $GSD_SDK query commit "docs: capture todo - [title]" --files .planning/todos/pending/[filename] .planning/STATE.md
136: ```
137: 
138: Tool respects `commit_docs` config and gitignore automatically.
139: 
140: Confirm: "Committed: docs: capture todo - [title]"
141: </step>
142: 
143: <step name="confirm">
144: ```
145: Todo saved: .planning/todos/pending/[filename]
146: 
147:   [title]
148:   Area: [area]
149:   Files: [count] referenced
150: 
151: ---
152: 
153: Would you like to:
154: 
155: 1. Continue with current work
156: 2. Add another todo
157: 3. View all todos (/gsd-capture --list)
158: ```
159: </step>
160: 
161: </process>
162: 
163: <success_criteria>
164: - [ ] Directory structure exists
165: - [ ] Todo file created with valid frontmatter
166: - [ ] Problem section has enough context for future the agent
167: - [ ] No duplicates (checked and resolved)
168: - [ ] Area consistent with existing todos
169: - [ ] STATE.md updated if exists
170: - [ ] Todo and state committed to git
171: </success_criteria>

(End of file - total 171 lines)
</content>
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/note.md"}
<path>/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/note.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: <purpose>
2: Zero-friction idea capture. One Write call, one confirmation line. No questions, no prompts.
3: 
4: **Text mode (`workflow.text_mode: true` in config or `--text` flag):** Set `TEXT_MODE=true` if `--text` is present in `$ARGUMENTS` OR `text_mode` from init JSON is `true`. When TEXT_MODE is active, replace every `question` call with a plain-text numbered list and ask the user to type their choice number. This is required for non-the agent runtimes (OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) where `question` is not available.
5: Runs inline — no Task, no question, no Bash.
6: </purpose>
7: 
8: <required_reading>
9: Read all files referenced by the invoking prompt's execution_context before starting.
10: </required_reading>
11: 
12: <process>
13: 
14: <step name="storage_format">
15: **Note storage format.**
16: 
17: Notes are stored as individual markdown files:
18: 
19: - **Project scope**: `.planning/notes/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}.md` — used when `.planning/` exists in cwd
20: - **Global scope**: `/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/notes/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}.md` — fallback when no `.planning/`, or when `--global` flag is present
21: 
22: Each note file:
23: 
24: ```markdown
25: ---
26: date: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm"
27: promoted: false
28: ---
29: 
30: {note text verbatim}
31: ```
32: 
33: **`--global` flag**: Strip `--global` from anywhere in `$ARGUMENTS` before parsing. When present, force global scope regardless of whether `.planning/` exists.
34: 
35: **Important**: Do NOT create `.planning/` if it doesn't exist. Fall back to global scope silently.
36: </step>
37: 
38: <step name="parse_subcommand">
39: **Parse subcommand from $ARGUMENTS (after stripping --global).**
40: 
41: | Condition | Subcommand |
42: |-----------|------------|
43: | Arguments are exactly `list` (case-insensitive) | **list** |
44: | Arguments are exactly `promote <N>` where N is a number | **promote** |
45: | Arguments are empty (no text at all) | **list** |
46: | Anything else | **append** (the text IS the note) |
47: 
48: **Critical**: `list` is only a subcommand when it's the ENTIRE argument. `/gsd-note list of groceries` saves a note with text "list of groceries". Same for `promote` — only a subcommand when followed by exactly one number.
49: </step>
50: 
51: <step name="append">
52: **Subcommand: append — create a timestamped note file.**
53: 
54: 1. Determine scope (project or global) per storage format above
55: 2. Ensure the notes directory exists (`.planning/notes/` or `/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/notes/`)
56: 3. Generate slug: first ~4 meaningful words of the note text, lowercase, hyphen-separated (strip articles/prepositions from the start)
57: 4. Generate filename: `{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}.md`
58:    - If a file with that name already exists, append `-2`, `-3`, etc.
59: 5. Write the file with frontmatter and note text (see storage format)
60: 6. Confirm with exactly one line: `Noted ({scope}): {note text}`
61:    - Where `{scope}` is "project" or "global"
62: 
63: **Constraints:**
64: - **Never modify the note text** — capture verbatim, including typos
65: - **Never ask questions** — just write and confirm
66: - **Timestamp format**: Use local time, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm` (24-hour, no seconds)
67: </step>
68: 
69: <step name="list">
70: **Subcommand: list — show notes from both scopes.**
71: 
72: 1. Glob `.planning/notes/*.md` (if directory exists) — project notes
73: 2. Glob `/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/notes/*.md` (if directory exists) — global notes
74: 3. For each file, read frontmatter to get `date` and `promoted` status
75: 4. Exclude files where `promoted: true` from active counts (but still show them, dimmed)
76: 5. Sort by date, number all active entries sequentially starting at 1
77: 6. If total active entries > 20, show only the last 10 with a note about how many were omitted
78: 
79: **Display format:**
80: 
81: ```
82: Notes:
83: 
84: Project (.planning/notes/):
85:   1. [2026-02-08 14:32] refactor the hook system to support async validators
86:   2. [promoted] [2026-02-08 14:40] add rate limiting to the API endpoints
87:   3. [2026-02-08 15:10] consider adding a --dry-run flag to build
88: 
89: Global (/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/notes/):
90:   4. [2026-02-08 10:00] cross-project idea about shared config
91: 
92: {count} active note(s). Use `/gsd-note promote <N>` to convert to a todo.
93: ```
94: 
95: If a scope has no directory or no entries, show: `(no notes)`
96: </step>
97: 
98: <step name="promote">
99: **Subcommand: promote — convert a note into a todo.**
100: 
101: 1. Run the **list** logic to build the numbered index (both scopes)
102: 2. Find entry N from the numbered list
103: 3. If N is invalid or refers to an already-promoted note, tell the user and stop
104: 4. **Requires `.planning/` directory** — if it doesn't exist, warn: "Todos require a GSD project. Run `/gsd-new-project` to initialize one."
105: 5. Ensure `.planning/todos/pending/` directory exists
106: 6. Generate todo ID: `{NNN}-{slug}` where NNN is the next sequential number (scan both `.planning/todos/pending/` and `.planning/todos/completed/` for the highest existing number, increment by 1, zero-pad to 3 digits) and slug is the first ~4 meaningful words of the note text
107: 7. Extract the note text from the source file (body after frontmatter)
108: 8. Create `.planning/todos/pending/{id}.md`:
109: 
110: ```yaml
111: ---
112: title: "{note text}"
113: status: pending
114: priority: P2
115: source: "promoted from /gsd-note"
116: created: {YYYY-MM-DD}
117: theme: general
118: ---
119: 
120: ## Goal
121: 
122: {note text}
123: 
124: ## Context
125: 
126: Promoted from quick note captured on {original date}.
127: 
128: ## Acceptance Criteria
129: 
130: - [ ] {primary criterion derived from note text}
131: ```
132: 
133: 9. Mark the source note file as promoted: update its frontmatter to `promoted: true`
134: 10. Confirm: `Promoted note {N} to todo {id}: {note text}`
135: </step>
136: 
137: </process>
138: 
139: <edge_cases>
140: 1. **"list" as note text**: `/gsd-note list of things` saves note "list of things" (subcommand only when `list` is the entire arg)
141: 2. **No `.planning/`**: Falls back to global `/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/notes/` — works in any directory
142: 3. **Promote without project**: Warns that todos require `.planning/`, suggests `/gsd-new-project`
143: 4. **Large files**: `list` shows last 10 when >20 active entries
144: 5. **Duplicate slugs**: Append `-2`, `-3` etc. to filename if slug already used on same date
145: 6. **`--global` position**: Stripped from anywhere — `--global my idea` and `my idea --global` both save "my idea" globally
146: 7. **Promote already-promoted**: Tell user "Note {N} is already promoted" and stop
147: 8. **Empty note text after stripping flags**: Treat as `list` subcommand
148: </edge_cases>
149: 
150: <success_criteria>
151: - [ ] Append: Note file written with correct frontmatter and verbatim text
152: - [ ] Append: No questions asked — instant capture
153: - [ ] List: Both scopes shown with sequential numbering
154: - [ ] List: Promoted notes shown but dimmed
155: - [ ] Promote: Todo created with correct format
156: - [ ] Promote: Source note marked as promoted
157: - [ ] Global fallback: Works when no `.planning/` exists
158: </success_criteria>

(End of file - total 158 lines)
</content>
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/add-backlog.md"}
<path>/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/add-backlog.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Add Backlog Item Workflow
2: 
3: Invoked by `/gsd-capture --backlog` (`commands/gsd/capture.md`).
4: 
5: Adds an idea to the ROADMAP.md backlog parking lot using 999.x numbering. Backlog items
6: are unsequenced ideas that aren't ready for active planning — they live outside the normal
7: phase sequence and accumulate context over time.
8: 
9: <process>
10: 
11: ## Step 1: Read ROADMAP.md
12: 
13: Check for existing backlog entries:
14: 
15: ```bash
16: cat .planning/ROADMAP.md
17: ```
18: 
19: ## Step 2: Find next backlog number
20: 
21: ```bash
22: # SDK resolution: prefer local gsd-tools.cjs, fall back to global gsd-sdk (#3668)
23: GSD_TOOLS="${RUNTIME_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)}/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs"
24: if [ -f "$GSD_TOOLS" ]; then
25:   GSD_SDK="node $GSD_TOOLS"
26: elif command -v gsd-sdk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
27:   GSD_SDK="gsd-sdk"
28: else
29:   echo "ERROR: gsd-sdk not found on PATH and $GSD_TOOLS does not exist." >&2
30:   echo "Run: npx get-shit-done-cc@latest --claude --local" >&2
31:   exit 1
32: fi
33: NEXT=$($GSD_SDK query phase.next-decimal 999 --raw)
34: ```
35: 
36: If no 999.x phases exist yet, `phase.next-decimal` returns `999.1`. Sparse numbering
37: is fine (e.g. 999.1, 999.3) — always use `phase.next-decimal`, never guess.
38: 
39: ## Step 3: Write ROADMAP entry
40: 
41: **Write the ROADMAP entry BEFORE creating the directory.** Directory existence is a
42: reliable indicator that the phase is already registered, which prevents false duplicate
43: detection in any hook that checks for existing 999.x directories (#2280).
44: 
45: Add under a `## Backlog` section. If the section doesn't exist, create it at the end
46: of ROADMAP.md:
47: 
48: ```markdown
49: ## Backlog
50: 
51: ### Phase {NEXT}: {description} (BACKLOG)
52: 
53: **Goal:** [Captured for future planning]
54: **Requirements:** TBD
55: **Plans:** 0 plans
56: 
57: Plans:
58: - [ ] TBD (promote with /gsd-review-backlog when ready)
59: ```
60: 
61: ## Step 4: Create the phase directory
62: 
63: Apply the `project_code` prefix (if set in `.planning/config.json`) so the backlog directory name is consistent with all other phase-creation paths:
64: 
65: ```bash
66: SLUG=$($GSD_SDK query generate-slug "$ARGUMENTS" --raw)
67: PROJECT_CODE=$($GSD_SDK query config-get project_code --raw 2>/dev/null || echo "")
68: PREFIX=$([ -n "$PROJECT_CODE" ] && echo "${PROJECT_CODE}-" || echo "")
69: PHASE_DIR=".planning/phases/${PREFIX}${NEXT}-${SLUG}"
70: mkdir -p "${PHASE_DIR}"
71: touch "${PHASE_DIR}/.gitkeep"
72: ```
73: 
74: ## Step 5: Commit
75: 
76: ```bash
77: $GSD_SDK query commit "docs: add backlog item ${NEXT} — ${ARGUMENTS}" --files .planning/ROADMAP.md "${PHASE_DIR}/.gitkeep"
78: ```
79: 
80: ## Step 6: Report
81: 
82: ```
83: ## 📋 Backlog Item Added
84: 
85: Phase {NEXT}: {description}
86: Directory: {PHASE_DIR}/
87: 
88: This item lives in the backlog parking lot.
89: Use /gsd-discuss-phase {NEXT} to explore it further.
90: Use /gsd-review-backlog to promote items to active milestone.
91: ```
92: 
93: </process>
94: 
95: <notes>
96: - 999.x numbering keeps backlog items out of the active phase sequence
97: - Phase directories are created immediately so /gsd-discuss-phase and /gsd-plan-phase work on them
98: - No `Depends on:` field — backlog items are unsequenced by definition
99: - Sparse numbering is fine (999.1, 999.3) — always uses next-decimal
100: - Promote backlog items to the active milestone with /gsd-review-backlog
101: </notes>

(End of file - total 101 lines)
</content>
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/plant-seed.md"}
<path>/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/workflows/plant-seed.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: <purpose>
2: Capture a forward-looking idea as a structured seed file with trigger conditions.
3: Seeds auto-surface during /gsd-new-milestone when trigger conditions match the
4: new milestone's scope.
5: 
6: Seeds beat deferred items because they:
7: - Preserve WHY the idea matters (not just WHAT)
8: - Define WHEN to surface (trigger conditions, not manual scanning)
9: - Track breadcrumbs (code references, related decisions)
10: - Auto-present at the right time via new-milestone scan
11: 
12: **One-shot capture**: the seed file is written immediately from the idea text alone.
13: Trigger / Why / Scope are optional enrichment — they can be provided now or added
14: later. The file is never gated behind questions.
15: </purpose>
16: 
17: <process>
18: 
19: <step name="parse-idea">
20: Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for the idea summary.
21: 
22: First, check for an enrich flag:
23: 
24: ```bash
25: if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -qE '\-\-enrich[[:space:]]+SEED-[0-9]+'; then
26:   ENRICH_TARGET=$(echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -oE 'SEED-[0-9]+')
27:   SEED_FILE=$(ls .planning/seeds/${ENRICH_TARGET}-*.md 2>/dev/null | head -1)
28:   # Skip to enrich-seed step — do not prompt for $IDEA
29: else
30:   if [ -n "$ARGUMENTS" ]; then
31:     IDEA="$ARGUMENTS"
32:   else
33:     # Ask only when no arguments at all
34:     # What's the idea? (one sentence)
35:     IDEA="<user response>"
36:   fi
37: fi
38: ```
39: 
40: If `$ENRICH_TARGET` is set, skip straight to the `enrich-seed` step. Do not set `$IDEA` and do not run `create-seed-dir`, `generate-seed-id`, `write-seed`, `collect-breadcrumbs`, `commit-seed`, or `confirm`.
41: 
42: If `$ARGUMENTS` is non-empty and contains no `--enrich` flag, treat the full value as `$IDEA` (no prompt).
43: 
44: Only prompt for the idea when `$ARGUMENTS` is empty and no enrich target is present. Store the response as `$IDEA`.
45: </step>
46: 
47: <step name="create-seed-dir">
48: ```bash
49: mkdir -p .planning/seeds
50: ```
51: </step>
52: 
53: <step name="generate-seed-id">
54: ```bash
55: # Find next seed number
56: EXISTING=$( (ls .planning/seeds/SEED-*.md 2>/dev/null || true) | wc -l )
57: NEXT=$((EXISTING + 1))
58: PADDED=$(printf "%03d" $NEXT)
59: ```
60: 
61: Generate slug from idea summary.
62: </step>
63: 
64: <step name="write-seed">
65: Write `.planning/seeds/SEED-{PADDED}-{slug}.md` immediately with sensible defaults:
66: 
67: - `trigger_when`: default is `"when relevant"` — the seed will surface during any
68:   new-milestone scan; the user can narrow it later via `--enrich`
69: - `scope`: default is `"unknown"` — the user can update it via `--enrich`
70: 
71: ```markdown
72: ---
73: id: SEED-{PADDED}
74: status: dormant
75: planted: {ISO date}
76: planted_during: {current milestone/phase from STATE.md, or "unknown" if not in a GSD project}
77: trigger_when: when relevant
78: scope: unknown
79: ---
80: 
81: # SEED-{PADDED}: {$IDEA}
82: 
83: ## Why This Matters
84: 
85: _To be filled in. Run `/gsd-capture --seed --enrich SEED-{PADDED}` to add context._
86: 
87: ## When to Surface
88: 
89: **Trigger:** when relevant
90: 
91: This seed will surface during `/gsd-new-milestone` when the milestone scope matches.
92: 
93: ## Scope Estimate
94: 
95: **Unknown** — run `/gsd-capture --seed --enrich SEED-{PADDED}` to estimate effort.
96: 
97: ## Breadcrumbs
98: 
99: _No breadcrumbs collected yet._
100: 
101: ## Notes
102: 
103: _Captured via one-shot seed capture. Enrich with trigger, why, and scope at your convenience._
104: ```
105: </step>
106: 
107: <step name="collect-breadcrumbs">
108: After writing the file, search the codebase for relevant references:
109: 
110: Extract one or two key terms from `$IDEA` (the most distinctive noun or phrase) and store as `$KEYWORD`.
111: 
112: ```bash
113: # Derive a single keyword for breadcrumb search.
114: # Lower-case, strip punctuation, take the first token longer than 2 chars.
115: KEYWORD=$(printf '%s' "$IDEA" \
116:   | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' \
117:   | tr -cs 'a-z0-9' '\n' \
118:   | awk 'length > 2 {print; exit}')
119: KEYWORD="${KEYWORD:-seed}"  # fallback to literal "seed" if extraction yields nothing
120: ```
121: 
122: ```bash
123: # Find files related to the idea keywords ($KEYWORD derived from $IDEA)
124: grep -rl "$KEYWORD" --include="*.ts" --include="*.js" --include="*.md" . 2>/dev/null | head -10
125: ```
126: 
127: Also check:
128: - Current STATE.md for related decisions
129: - ROADMAP.md for related phases
130: - todos/ for related captured ideas
131: 
132: If any breadcrumbs are found, update the Breadcrumbs section of the seed file.
133: Store relevant file paths as `$BREADCRUMBS`.
134: </step>
135: 
136: <step name="commit-seed">
137: ```bash
138: # SDK resolution: prefer local gsd-tools.cjs, fall back to global gsd-sdk (#3668)
139: GSD_TOOLS="${RUNTIME_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)}/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs"
140: if [ -f "$GSD_TOOLS" ]; then
141:   GSD_SDK="node $GSD_TOOLS"
142: elif command -v gsd-sdk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
143:   GSD_SDK="gsd-sdk"
144: else
145:   echo "ERROR: gsd-sdk not found on PATH and $GSD_TOOLS does not exist." >&2
146:   echo "Run: npx get-shit-done-cc@latest --claude --local" >&2
147:   exit 1
148: fi
149: $GSD_SDK query commit "docs: plant seed — {$IDEA}" --files .planning/seeds/SEED-{PADDED}-{slug}.md
150: ```
151: </step>
152: 
153: <step name="confirm">
154: ```text
155: ✅ Seed planted: SEED-{PADDED}
156: 
157: "{$IDEA}"
158: File: .planning/seeds/SEED-{PADDED}-{slug}.md
159: 
160: Trigger and scope are set to defaults. Run `/gsd-capture --seed --enrich SEED-{PADDED}`
161: to add trigger conditions, rationale, and scope estimate at your convenience.
162: 
163: This seed will surface automatically when you run /gsd-new-milestone.
164: ```
165: </step>
166: 
167: <step name="enrich-seed">
168: **Optional enrichment — only run this step when `--enrich` flag is present.**
169: 
170: If `--enrich` flag is in `$ARGUMENTS`:
171: - `$ENRICH_TARGET` and `$SEED_FILE` are already set by `parse-idea`. Derive `$SEED_ID` from `$ENRICH_TARGET` (e.g. `SEED_ID="$ENRICH_TARGET"`). If `$SEED_FILE` is empty, fall back to the most-recently modified file in `.planning/seeds/` and set `$SEED_ID` from its filename.
172: - Ask focused questions to build a complete seed:
173: 
174: 
175: **Text mode (`workflow.text_mode: true` in config or `--text` flag):** Set `TEXT_MODE=true` if `--text` is present in `$ARGUMENTS` OR `text_mode` from init JSON is `true`. When TEXT_MODE is active, replace every `question` call with a plain-text numbered list and ask the user to type their choice number. This is required for non-the agent runtimes (OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) where `question` is not available.
176: 
177: ```text
178: question(
179:   header: "Trigger",
180:   question: "When should this idea surface? (e.g., 'when we add user accounts', 'next major version', 'when performance becomes a priority')",
181:   options: []  // freeform
182: )
183: ```
184: 
185: Store as `$TRIGGER`.
186: 
187: ```text
188: question(
189:   header: "Why",
190:   question: "Why does this matter? What problem does it solve or what opportunity does it create?",
191:   options: []
192: )
193: ```
194: 
195: Store as `$WHY`.
196: 
197: ```text
198: question(
199:   header: "Scope",
200:   question: "How big is this? (rough estimate)",
201:   options: [
202:     { label: "Small", description: "A few hours — could be a quick task" },
203:     { label: "Medium", description: "A phase or two — needs planning" },
204:     { label: "Large", description: "A full milestone — significant effort" }
205:   ]
206: )
207: ```
208: 
209: Store as `$SCOPE`.
210: 
211: Update the seed file's frontmatter and sections with the gathered values:
212: - Set `trigger_when: {$TRIGGER}`
213: - Set `scope: {$SCOPE}`
214: - Fill in `## Why This Matters` with `{$WHY}`
215: - Fill in `## When to Surface` trigger detail
216: - Fill in `## Scope Estimate` elaboration
217: 
218: Commit the update:
219: ```bash
220: $GSD_SDK query commit "docs: enrich seed ${SEED_ID} — trigger + why + scope" --files "$SEED_FILE"
221: ```
222: 
223: Confirm:
224: ```text
225: ✅ Seed enriched: ${SEED_ID}
226: Trigger: {$TRIGGER}
227: Scope: {$SCOPE}
228: ```
229: </step>
230: 
231: </process>
232: 
233: <success_criteria>
234: - [ ] Seed file created in .planning/seeds/ in one step, no questions required
235: - [ ] Frontmatter includes status, trigger_when (default: "when relevant"), scope (default: "unknown")
236: - [ ] File is written BEFORE any optional enrichment questions are asked
237: - [ ] Committed to git
238: - [ ] User shown confirmation with file path
239: - [ ] Optional --enrich path available for adding trigger, why, scope post-capture
240: </success_criteria>

(End of file - total 240 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: <purpose>
2: List all pending todos, allow selection, load full context for the selected todo, and route to appropriate action.
3: </purpose>
4: 
5: <required_reading>
6: Read all files referenced by the invoking prompt's execution_context before starting.
7: </required_reading>
8: 
9: <process>
10: 
11: <step name="init_context">
12: Load todo context:
13: 
14: ```bash
15: # SDK resolution: prefer local gsd-tools.cjs, fall back to global gsd-sdk (#3668)
16: GSD_TOOLS="${RUNTIME_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)}/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs"
17: if [ -f "$GSD_TOOLS" ]; then
18:   GSD_SDK="node $GSD_TOOLS"
19: elif command -v gsd-sdk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
20:   GSD_SDK="gsd-sdk"
21: else
22:   echo "ERROR: gsd-sdk not found on PATH and $GSD_TOOLS does not exist." >&2
23:   echo "Run: npx get-shit-done-cc@latest --claude --local" >&2
24:   exit 1
25: fi
26: INIT=$($GSD_SDK query init.todos)
27: if [[ "$INIT" == @file:* ]]; then INIT=$(cat "${INIT#@file:}"); fi
28: ```
29: 
30: Extract from init JSON: `todo_count`, `todos`, `pending_dir`.
31: 
32: If `todo_count` is 0:
33: ```
34: No pending todos.
35: 
36: Todos are captured during work sessions with /gsd-add-todo.
37: 
38: ---
39: 
40: Would you like to:
41: 
42: 1. Continue with current phase (/gsd-progress)
43: 2. Add a todo now (/gsd-add-todo)
44: ```
45: 
46: Exit.
47: </step>
48: 
49: <step name="parse_filter">
50: Check for area filter in arguments:
51: - `/gsd-capture --list` → show all
52: - `/gsd-capture --list api` → filter to area:api only
53: </step>
54: 
55: <step name="list_todos">
56: Use the `todos` array from init context (already filtered by area if specified).
57: 
58: Parse and display as numbered list:
59: 
60: ```
61: Pending Todos:
62: 
63: 1. Add auth token refresh (api, 2d ago)
64: 2. Fix modal z-index issue (ui, 1d ago)
65: 3. Refactor database connection pool (database, 5h ago)
66: 
67: ---
68: 
69: Reply with a number to view details, or:
70: - `/gsd-capture --list [area]` to filter by area
71: - `q` to exit
72: ```
73: 
74: Format age as relative time from created timestamp.
75: </step>
76: 
77: <step name="handle_selection">
78: Wait for user to reply with a number.
79: 
80: If valid: load selected todo, proceed.
81: If invalid: "Invalid selection. Reply with a number (1-[N]) or `q` to exit."
82: </step>
83: 
84: <step name="load_context">
85: Read the todo file completely. Display:
86: 
87: ```
88: ## [title]
89: 
90: **Area:** [area]
91: **Created:** [date] ([relative time] ago)
92: **Files:** [list or "None"]
93: 
94: ### Problem
95: [problem section content]
96: 
97: ### Solution
98: [solution section content]
99: ```
100: 
101: If `files` field has entries, read and briefly summarize each.
102: </step>
103: 
104: <step name="check_roadmap">
105: Check for roadmap (can use init progress or directly check file existence):
106: 
107: If `.planning/ROADMAP.md` exists:
108: 1. Check if todo's area matches an upcoming phase
109: 2. Check if todo's files overlap with a phase's scope
110: 3. Note any match for action options
111: </step>
112: 
113: <step name="offer_actions">
114: **If todo maps to a roadmap phase:**
115: 
116: 
117: **Text mode (`workflow.text_mode: true` in config or `--text` flag):** Set `TEXT_MODE=true` if `--text` is present in `$ARGUMENTS` OR `text_mode` from init JSON is `true`. When TEXT_MODE is active, replace every `question` call with a plain-text numbered list and ask the user to type their choice number. This is required for non-the agent runtimes (OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) where `question` is not available.
118: Use question:
119: - header: "Action"
120: - question: "This todo relates to Phase [N]: [name]. What would you like to do?"
121: - options:
122:   - "Work on it now" — move to done, start working
123:   - "Add to phase plan" — include when planning Phase [N]
124:   - "Brainstorm approach" — think through before deciding
125:   - "Put it back" — return to list
126: 
127: **If no roadmap match:**
128: 
129: Use question:
130: - header: "Action"
131: - question: "What would you like to do with this todo?"
132: - options:
133:   - "Work on it now" — move to done, start working
134:   - "Create a phase" — /gsd-add-phase with this scope
135:   - "Brainstorm approach" — think through before deciding
136:   - "Put it back" — return to list
137: </step>
138: 
139: <step name="execute_action">
140: **Work on it now:**
141: ```bash
142: mv ".planning/todos/pending/[filename]" ".planning/todos/completed/"
143: ```
144: Update STATE.md todo count. Present problem/solution context. Begin work or ask how to proceed.
145: 
146: **Add to phase plan:**
147: Note todo reference in phase planning notes. Keep in pending. Return to list or exit.
148: 
149: **Create a phase:**
150: Display: `/gsd-add-phase [description from todo]`
151: Keep in pending. User runs command in fresh context.
152: 
153: **Brainstorm approach:**
154: Keep in pending. Start discussion about problem and approaches.
155: 
156: **Put it back:**
157: Return to list_todos step.
158: </step>
159: 
160: <step name="update_state">
161: After any action that changes todo count:
162: 
163: Re-run `init todos` to get updated count, then update STATE.md "### Pending Todos" section if exists.
164: </step>
165: 
166: <step name="git_commit">
167: If todo was moved to done/, commit the change:
168: 
169: ```bash
170: git rm --cached .planning/todos/pending/[filename] 2>/dev/null || true
171: $GSD_SDK query commit "docs: start work on todo - [title]" --files .planning/todos/completed/[filename] .planning/STATE.md
172: ```
173: 
174: Tool respects `commit_docs` config and gitignore automatically.
175: 
176: Confirm: "Committed: docs: start work on todo - [title]"
177: </step>
178: 
179: </process>
180: 
181: <success_criteria>
182: - [ ] All pending todos listed with title, area, age
183: - [ ] Area filter applied if specified
184: - [ ] Selected todo's full context loaded
185: - [ ] Roadmap context checked for phase match
186: - [ ] Appropriate actions offered
187: - [ ] Selected action executed
188: - [ ] STATE.md updated if todo count changed
189: - [ ] Changes committed to git (if todo moved to done/)
190: </success_criteria>

(End of file - total 190 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin-private/.opencode/get-shit-done/references/ui-brand.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: <ui_patterns>
2: 
3: Visual patterns for user-facing GSD output. Orchestrators @-reference this file.
4: 
5: ## Stage Banners
6: 
7: Use for major workflow transitions.
8: 
9: ```
10: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
11:  GSD ► {STAGE NAME}
12: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
13: ```
14: 
15: **Stage names (uppercase):**
16: - `QUESTIONING`
17: - `RESEARCHING`
18: - `DEFINING REQUIREMENTS`
19: - `CREATING ROADMAP`
20: - `PLANNING PHASE {N}`
21: - `EXECUTING WAVE {N}`
22: - `VERIFYING`
23: - `PHASE {N} COMPLETE ✓`
24: - `MILESTONE COMPLETE 🎉`
25: 
26: ---
27: 
28: ## Checkpoint Boxes
29: 
30: User action required. 62-character width.
31: 
32: ```
33: ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
34: ║  CHECKPOINT: {Type}                                          ║
35: ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
36: 
37: {Content}
38: 
39: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
40: → {ACTION PROMPT}
41: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42: ```
43: 
44: **Types:**
45: - `CHECKPOINT: Verification Required` → `→ Type "approved" or describe issues`
46: - `CHECKPOINT: Decision Required` → `→ Select: option-a / option-b`
47: - `CHECKPOINT: Action Required` → `→ Type "done" when complete`
48: 
49: ---
50: 
51: ## Status Symbols
52: 
53: ```
54: ✓  Complete / Passed / Verified
55: ✗  Failed / Missing / Blocked
56: ◆  In Progress
57: ○  Pending
58: ⚡ Auto-approved
59: ⚠  Warning
60: 🎉 Milestone complete (only in banner)
61: ```
62: 
63: ---
64: 
65: ## Progress Display
66: 
67: **Phase/milestone level:**
68: ```
69: Progress: ████████░░ 80%
70: ```
71: 
72: **Task level:**
73: ```
74: Tasks: 2/4 complete
75: ```
76: 
77: **Plan level:**
78: ```
79: Plans: 3/5 complete
80: ```
81: 
82: ---
83: 
84: ## Spawning Indicators
85: 
86: ```
87: ◆ Spawning researcher...
88: 
89: ◆ Spawning 4 researchers in parallel...
90:   → Stack research
91:   → Features research
92:   → Architecture research
93:   → Pitfalls research
94: 
95: ✓ Researcher complete: STACK.md written
96: ```
97: 
98: ---
99: 
100: ## Next Up Block
101: 
102: Always at end of major completions.
103: 
104: ```
105: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
106: 
107: ## ▶ Next Up
108: 
109: **{Identifier}: {Name}** — {one-line description}
110: 
111: `/clear` then:
112: 
113: `{copy-paste command}`
114: 
115: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
116: 
117: **Also available:**
118: - `/gsd-alternative-1` — description
119: - `/gsd-alternative-2` — description
120: 
121: ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
122: ```
123: 
124: ---
125: 
126: ## Error Box
127: 
128: ```
129: ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
130: ║  ERROR                                                       ║
131: ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
132: 
133: {Error description}
134: 
135: **To fix:** {Resolution steps}
136: ```
137: 
138: ---
139: 
140: ## Tables
141: 
142: ```
143: | Phase | Status | Plans | Progress |
144: |-------|--------|-------|----------|
145: | 1     | ✓      | 3/3   | 100%     |
146: | 2     | ◆      | 1/4   | 25%      |
147: | 3     | ○      | 0/2   | 0%       |
148: ```
149: 
150: ---
151: 
152: ## Anti-Patterns
153: 
154: - Varying box/banner widths
155: - Mixing banner styles (`===`, `---`, `***`)
156: - Skipping `GSD ►` prefix in banners
157: - Random emoji (`🚀`, `✨`, `💫`)
158: - Missing Next Up block after completions
159: 
160: </ui_patterns>

(End of file - total 160 lines)
</content>
